sentence: Num vai chové hoje, que o céu tá limpo.
ipa: ˈnũ ˈvaj ʃoˈvɛ ˈoʒi ˈki ˈu kɛˈu ˈta ˈlĩpu
gloss_en: It's not going to rain today, the sky is clear.
notes: Final-/r/ elision in 'chové' (chover) per amaral1920 §23a; nasalised negator 'num' and 'que o' liaison per amaral1920; general BR reduction per silva2002.

sentence: Essa dúzia de ovo custa oito real na feira.
ipa: ˈesɐ ˈduziɐ ˈdi ˈovu ˈkustɐ ˈojtu heˈaw ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: That dozen eggs costs eight reais at the market.
notes: Onset /r/ realised as glottal [h] in 'real' and 'ei' diphthong in 'feira' per amaral1920 §6; non-agreeing colloquial 'oito real' per castilho2010; coda /l/→[w] in 'real' per silva2002.

sentence: A consulta é logo cedo, lá pras sete da manhã.
ipa: ˈɐ kõˈsuwtɐ ˈɛ ˈloɡu ˈs̺edu ˈla ˈpɾas ˈseti ˈdɐ maˈɲɐ̃
gloss_en: The appointment is early, around seven in the morning.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'manhã' and open [ɛ] in 'é' per amaral1920; coda /l/→[w] in 'consulta' per silva2002; unstressed reduction per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Meu tio véio mora perto do córrego.
ipa: ˈmew ˈt͡ʃiu ˈvɛiu ˈmoɾɐ ˈpeɻtu ˈdu ˈkɔʁeɡu
gloss_en: My old uncle lives near the stream.
notes: /ʎ/ vocalised to [j] in 'véio' (velho) per amaral1920 §25; retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'perto' and strong onset [ʁ] in 'córrego' per amaral1920 §6b.

sentence: Vira ali na porteira e segue reto até o rio.
ipa: ˈviɾɐ aˈli ˈnɐ poˈɻtejɾɐ ˈi ˈseɡi ˈhetu aˈtɛ ˈu ˈhiu
gloss_en: Turn there at the gate and go straight to the river.
notes: Retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'porteira' and glottal onset [h] in 'reto'/'rio' per amaral1920 §6b; 'segue reto' and 'até o' liaisons per silva2002; 'ei' in 'porteira'.

sentence: Essa carne de porco tava gostosa demais.
ipa: ˈesɐ ˈkaɻni ˈdi ˈpoɻku ˈtavɐ ɡoˈstozɐ deˈmajs
gloss_en: That pork meat was really tasty.
notes: Retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'carne'/'porco' per amaral1920 §6b; laminodental coda /s/ [s] with no chiado per amaral1920 §6a.

sentence: Podia me falá onde qu'ocê achô esse chapéu?
ipa: poˈd͡ʒiɐ ˈmi faˈla ˈõdi ˈk oˈke aˈʃo ˈesi ʃapɛˈu
gloss_en: Could you tell me where you found that hat?
notes: Final-/r/ elision in 'falá' and aphaeretic 3sg apocope 'achô' (achou) per amaral1920 §23a; nasal [õ] in 'onde' and 'onde qu'ocê' liaison per silva2002.

sentence: Quantos saco de mio ocê carregô na carroça?
ipa: ˈkɐ̃tos ˈsaku ˈdi ˈmiu oˈke kaʁeˈɡo ˈnɐ kaˈʁosɐ
gloss_en: How many sacks of corn did you carry on the cart?
notes: /ʎ/ vocalised to [j] in 'mio' (milho) per amaral1920 §25; non-agreeing 'quantos saco' per castilho2010; strong onset [ʁ] in 'carroça' per amaral1920 §6.

sentence: Se chovesse mais, a roça dava um feijão bão.
ipa: ˈsi ʃoˈvesi ˈmajs ˈɐ ˈhosɐ ˈdavɐ ˈũ feˈjʒɐ̃w̃ ˈbɐ̃w̃
gloss_en: If it rained more, the field would give good beans.
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'feijão'/'bão' and glottal onset [h] in 'roça' per amaral1920 §6; caipira nasalised 'bão' (bom) per mateus_dandrade2000; 'ei' in 'feijão'.

sentence: Ontem eu comprei arroz, farinha e um pedaço de carne seca.
ipa: ˈõtẽ ˈew kõˈpɾej aˈʁos faˈɾiɲɐ ˈi ˈũ peˈdasu ˈdi ˈkaɻni ˈsekɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought rice, flour and a piece of jerked meat.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'farinha', strong onset [ʁ] in 'arroz' and 'ei' in 'comprei' per amaral1920 §6; 'ontem eu' and 'farinha e' liaisons per silva2002.

sentence: Amanhã nóis vai lá pra cidade de trem.
ipa: amaˈɲɐ̃ nɔˈis ˈvaj ˈla ˈpɾɐ s̺iˈdadi ˈdi ˈtɾẽ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we're going to the city by train.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' per amaral1920; non-agreeing colloquial subject 'nóis vai' per castilho2010; nasal [ẽ] in 'trem' per silva2002.

sentence: Me liga quando cê chegá na venda do seu Zé.
ipa: ˈmi ˈliɡɐ ˈkɐ̃du ˈke ʃeˈɡa ˈnɐ ˈvẽdɐ ˈdu ˈsew ˈzɛ
gloss_en: Call me when you get to Zé's shop.
notes: Final-/r/ elision in 'chegá' per amaral1920 §23a; reduced clitic 'cê' (você) and nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' per castilho2010.

sentence: Bão dia, e então, como qu'ocê tá passando?
ipa: ˈbɐ̃w̃ ˈd͡ʒiɐ ˈi ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkomu ˈk oˈke ˈta paˈsɐ̃du
gloss_en: Good morning, so how have you been?
notes: Caipira nasalised greeting 'bão dia' per mateus_dandrade2000; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' and 'e então' liaison per silva2002; nasal [ɐ̃] in 'passando'.

sentence: Quem num arrisca, num petisca, dizia meu avô.
ipa: ˈkẽ ˈnũ aˈʁiskɐ ˈnũ peˈt͡ʃiskɐ d͡ʒiˈziɐ ˈmew aˈvo
gloss_en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained, my grandfather used to say.
notes: Nasalised negator 'num' and strong onset [ʁ] in 'arrisca' per amaral1920 §6; /t/ palatalisation [t͡ʃ] in 'petisca' per silva2002; proverb register per callou_leite2001.

sentence: O carro véio do vizinho quebrô na estrada de terra.
ipa: ˈu ˈkaʁu ˈvɛiu ˈdu viˈziɲu keˈbɾo ˈnɐ eˈstɾadɐ ˈdi ˈteʁɐ
gloss_en: The neighbour's old car broke down on the dirt road.
notes: /ʎ/ vocalised to [j] in 'véio' per amaral1920 §25; palatal [ɲ] in 'vizinho', strong onset [ʁ] in 'carro'/'terra' per amaral1920 §6; apocope 'quebrô' (quebrou) per silva2002.

sentence: Aquele boi vermeio bebeu água no coxo de madeira.
ipa: aˈkeli ˈboj veˈɻmeju beˈbew ˈaɡɐ ˈnu ˈkoʃu ˈdi maˈdejɾɐ
gloss_en: That red ox drank water in the wooden trough.
notes: /ʎ/ vocalised to [j] in 'vermeio' (vermelho) per amaral1920 §25; 'ei' diphthong in 'madeira' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'no coxo' unstressed reduction per silva2002.

sentence: A muié dele fez um doce de leite bão de comê.
ipa: ˈɐ muˈjɛ ˈdeli ˈfes ˈũ ˈdos̺i ˈdi ˈlejti ˈbɐ̃w̃ ˈdi koˈme
gloss_en: His wife made a good milk sweet to eat.
notes: /ʎ/ vocalised to [j] in 'muié' (mulher) per amaral1920 §25; final-/r/ elision in 'comê' per amaral1920 §23a; 'ei' in 'leite', nasal [ɐ̃w̃] in 'bão'.

sentence: A ponte de madeira passa por riba do ribeirão.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈpõti ˈdi maˈdejɾɐ ˈpasɐ ˈpoɻ ˈhibɐ ˈdu hibeˈjɾɐ̃w̃
gloss_en: The wooden bridge crosses over the stream.
notes: Retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'por' and glottal onset [h] in 'riba'/'ribeirão' per amaral1920 §6b; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'ribeirão'; 'ei' in 'madeira'/'ribeirão'.

sentence: Ele pendura a carça e o chapéu atrás da porta.
ipa: ˈeli pẽˈduɾɐ ˈɐ ˈkaɻsɐ ˈi ˈu ʃapɛˈu aˈtɾas ˈdɐ ˈpoɻtɐ
gloss_en: He hangs the trousers and the hat behind the door.
notes: Coda /l/→/r/ rhotacism giving retroflex [ɻ] in 'carça' (calça) per amaral1920 §22a; retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'porta' per amaral1920 §6b; 'a carça e' liaison per silva2002.

sentence: O sol forte racha a terra seca do quintá.
ipa: ˈu ˈsow ˈfoɻti ˈhaʃɐ ˈɐ ˈteʁɐ ˈsekɐ ˈdu kĩˈta
gloss_en: The strong sun cracks the dry earth of the yard.
notes: Coda /l/→[w] in 'sol', retroflex coda /r/ [ɻ] in 'forte' and glottal onset [h] in 'racha' per amaral1920 §6; final-/r/ elision in 'quintá' (quintal→quintar→quintá) per amaral1920 §22a-23a; 'racha a' liaison.
